varieties.name: Linum perenne (Perennial Blue Flax) 🏆
varieties.origin: Native to Europe (Alps, England), 50+ year cultivation history
varieties.traits: Semi-evergreen perennial, 30-60cm tall, slender stems, narrow blue-green foliage
varieties.growing_notes: Heat, humidity and drought tolerant. Suitable for humid subtropical climates subtropical climate in well-drained soil with full sun to part shade. Self-seeds readily. Better humid subtropical climates performance than L. usitatissimum.
varieties.availability: Australian garden centers, The Seed Collection

varieties.name: Linum perenne 'Blau Saphir' (Sapphire Blue Flax) 🧬
varieties.traits: Compact growth, intense sapphire-blue flowers, longer bloom time than species
varieties.growing_notes: Excellent subtropical performance, one of the best ornamental flax for humid subtropical climates

varieties.name: Linum perenne 'Album' (White Perennial Flax) 🧬
varieties.traits: White-flowered variant, 25-30cm compact form
varieties.growing_notes: humid subtropical climates-suitable, prefers partial shade in hot areas

varieties.name: Linum lewisii (Lewis' Blue Flax / Prairie Flax) 🏆 🌱
varieties.origin: Native to western North America (Alaska to Baja California), discovered by Meriwether Lewis
varieties.traits: Short-lived perennial, 30-60cm tall, clumping habit, narrow green leaves
varieties.growing_notes: Very adaptable, drought, heat and cold tolerant. Attracts beneficial insects, bees, butterflies. Good subtropical performance. Often mistaken for L. perenne.
varieties.availability: Specialty native plant suppliers, heirloom seed companies

varieties.name: Linum narbonense (Narbonne Flax)
varieties.traits: Perennial, 30-75cm tall, whorl-structured gray-green leaves
varieties.growing_notes: Heat tolerant, suitable for humid subtropical climates rock gardens and borders in full sun with good drainage. Often considered superior to L. perenne for subtropical gardens.

varieties.name: Linum narbonense 'Heavenly Blue'
varieties.traits: Brighter, more vibrant blue flowers than species, improved performance
varieties.growing_notes: Excellent subtropical cultivar for humid subtropical climates

varieties.name: Linum grandiflorum 'Rubrum' (Scarlet Flax) 🏆
varieties.origin: Native to Algeria and Tunisia (North Africa), cultivated 50+ years
varieties.traits: Annual, 30-60cm tall, erect branching stems, narrow lanceolate leaves
varieties.growing_notes: Heat, humidity and drought tolerant once established. EXCELLENT for humid subtropical climates subtropical climate. Annual that readily self-seeds. RHS Award of Garden Merit. Re-seeds reliably without being invasive.
varieties.availability: Widely available in Australian seed suppliers

varieties.name: Linum grandiflorum 'Bright Eyes'
varieties.traits: White flowers with dark centers, 45cm tall, annual
varieties.growing_notes: Drought tolerant, suitable for humid subtropical climates containers and rock gardens

varieties.name: Linum flavum (Golden Flax / Yellow Flax)
varieties.origin: Native to central and southern Europe
varieties.traits: Compact perennial, 30cm tall, 20cm spread, woody structure, dark green foliage
varieties.growing_notes: Heat tolerant, suitable for humid subtropical climates in full sun with dry, well-drained soil

varieties.name: Linum marginale (Australian Native Flax) 🌱
varieties.traits: Short-lived perennial, 60cm tall, 30cm spread, clumping, sky-blue flowers
varieties.growing_notes: BEST NATIVE OPTION for humid subtropical climates. Tolerates frost, drought, most soil types. Dies back in late summer, reshoots. Suitable sun to part shade.

varieties.name: Phormium tenax (New Zealand Flax / Harakeke) 🌱
varieties.origin: Native to New Zealand and Norfolk Island
varieties.traits: Evergreen perennial, 1.8-3m tall, stiff upright sword-like leaves, gray-green to bronze-green, 6-15cm wide
varieties.growing_notes: EXCELLENT for humid subtropical climates subtropical climate. Tolerates heat, humidity, coastal conditions. Prefers moisture-retentive soil, full sun to partial shade. Used historically for fiber/linen production.
varieties.availability: Widely available in humid subtropical climates nurseries

varieties.name: Phormium tenax 'Variegatum'
varieties.traits: Green leaves striped with cream toward margins, 1.5m tall, RHS Award of Garden Merit
varieties.growing_notes: Excellent humid subtropical climates subtropical cultivar

varieties.name: Phormium tenax 'Sundowner'
varieties.traits: Erect bronze-green leaves striped with red and pink margins, 1.5m tall, RHS AGM
varieties.growing_notes: Outstanding color, humid subtropical climates-suitable

varieties.name: Phormium tenax 'Purpureum'
varieties.traits: Dark purple strap-shaped leaves, 1.2-1.8m tall and wide
varieties.growing_notes: Dramatic subtropical focal point for humid subtropical climates gardens

varieties.name: Phormium tenax 'Yellow Wave'
varieties.traits: Arching leaves heavily striped with bright gold, 3.6m tall, RHS AGM
varieties.growing_notes: Striking subtropical specimen for humid subtropical climates

varieties.name: Phormium tenax 'Duet'
varieties.traits: Green center leaves variegated with cream margins, 80cm tall compact form, RHS AGM
varieties.growing_notes: Ideal for smaller humid subtropical climates gardens

varieties.name: Phormium cookianum (Mountain Flax / Wharariki) 🌱
varieties.traits: Smaller, more graceful than P. tenax, medium-green leaves 1.2-1.5m long, 5-8cm wide, arching gracefully
varieties.growing_notes: EXCELLENT for humid subtropical climates. More water-requiring than P. tenax. Suitable for pond margins and water features. Can grow partially submerged.
varieties.availability: humid subtropical climates nurseries, increasingly popular

varieties.name: Phormium cookianum 'Cream Delight'
varieties.traits: Cream and green variegated foliage, arching habit
varieties.growing_notes: humid subtropical climates-suitable waterside plant

varieties.name: Phormium cookianum 'Tricolor'
varieties.traits: Green, cream and red striped leaves, multi-colored display
varieties.growing_notes: Eye-catching subtropical specimen for humid subtropical climates

varieties.name: Dianella caerulea (Blue Flax Lily) 🌱
varieties.origin: Native to eastern Australia including Queensland
varieties.traits: Evergreen perennial grass-like, strap-shaped leaves, purple flowers spring, blue berries
varieties.growing_notes: HIGHLY RECOMMENDED for humid subtropical climates subtropical gardens. Drought tolerant, adaptable to sun/shade, low maintenance. humid subtropical climates City Council recommended species.
varieties.availability: Widely available humid subtropical climates nurseries

varieties.name: Dianella 'Little Jess'
varieties.traits: Dwarf compact form, short canes that don't fall over, masses of purple flowers Sept-Nov
varieties.growing_notes: Exceptional drought tolerance, thrives in humid subtropical climates sun/shade, humidity and frost tolerant. Trim every 8 years.

varieties.name: Dianella 'Australiana'
varieties.traits: 60cm tall, blue flowers, drought resistant, erosion control
varieties.growing_notes: Excellent for humid subtropical climates subtropical climate, pollution tolerant, fast growing

varieties.name: Australian Linseed/Flaxseed 🏆
varieties.origin: Annual cultivated for seed/oil, centuries of cultivation
varieties.traits: Annual, 30-40cm tall, brown seeds, blue flowers spring-summer
varieties.growing_notes: Grown commercially in cool southern Australia (Victoria, South Australia). NOT suited to humid subtropical climates heat/humidity. Best in 450-750mm rainfall areas.

varieties.name: Glenelg 🏆
varieties.traits: Standard Victorian variety until fusarium wilt issues
varieties.growing_notes: Susceptible to fusarium wilt. Rarely grown in Victoria now, still grown northern NSW. NOT suitable humid subtropical climates.

varieties.name: Croxton
varieties.traits: Good fusarium wilt resistance, slightly lower yield than Glenelg, prone to lodging
varieties.growing_notes: Released to counter wilt problems in Glenelg. Cool-season only, NOT humid subtropical climates-suitable.

varieties.name: Linola™ (Solin)
varieties.origin: Developed by CSIRO Australia, trademark variety
varieties.traits: Golden yellow seeds (vs brown linseed), low alpha-linolenic acid oil similar to sunflower
varieties.growing_notes: Developed for edible oil market. Plants identical to linseed except seed color. Production at standstill in Victoria. Cool-season only, NOT humid subtropical climates-suitable.

varieties.name: T-397 🏆
varieties.traits: Brown seeds, suitable for both irrigated and rainfed areas
varieties.growing_notes: Popular across multiple Indian states. Rabi season crop (Oct-Nov sowing). NOT suitable for Tropical monsoon tropical monsoon climate.

varieties.name: JLS-23 (Jawahar-23) 🏆
varieties.origin: Jawahar series, developed for central India

varieties.name: Sheetal 🏆
varieties.traits: Plants up to 60cm tall (2 feet)

varieties.name: Kiran 🏆
varieties.traits: 36.6% oil content, lower linolenic acid (33.14%)

varieties.name: Jawahar-7 (R-7) 🏆
varieties.growing_notes: Rainfed/utera cultivation. Suitable for sowing before paddy harvest (utera system). Cool season only. NOT tropical monsoon climates-suitable.

varieties.name: Neelum (Neela) 🏆
varieties.traits: 34% oil content, lower linolenic acid (36.85%)

varieties.name: Indira Alsi-32
varieties.traits: 42.12% oil content

varieties.name: Deepika
varieties.traits: Highest oil content 42.27%, high linolenic acid 46.63%
varieties.growing_notes: Cool season. NOT tropical monsoon climates-suitable.

varieties.name: Kartika
varieties.traits: 41.11% oil content, 43.39% linolenic acid
varieties.growing_notes: Cool season. NOT tropical monsoon climates-suitable.

varieties.name: Shekhar
varieties.traits: 39.76% oil content, 46.13% linolenic acid
varieties.growing_notes: Cool season. NOT tropical monsoon climates-suitable.

varieties.name: Sharda
varieties.traits: 41.12% oil content, lower linolenic acid 34.41%
varieties.growing_notes: Cool season. NOT tropical monsoon climates-suitable.

varieties.name: Mayurbhanj 🏆 🌱
varieties.origin: Named after Mayurbhanj district, Odisha, India

varieties.name: RLC-92, RLC-122, RLC-134, GS-64
varieties.traits: High linolenic acid varieties (50-54%)
varieties.growing_notes: Cool season. NOT tropical monsoon climates-suitable.

varieties.name: Tiara (JRF-2)
varieties.origin: India's first dedicated fiber flax variety, released 2015
varieties.traits: Tall, unbranched stem for high-quality fiber, developed for linen production
varieties.growing_notes: Developed for sub-Himalayan tarai regions (Uttarakhand, Himachal Pradesh, UP plains, West Bengal). Shows great potential for Indian linen industry. Requires cool subtropical humid conditions. NOT suitable for Tropical monsoon tropical monsoon climate.

varieties.name: Linum grandiflorum 'Rubrum' (Scarlet Flax) 🏆
varieties.growing_notes: Best flax option for tropical monsoon climates. Heat and humidity tolerant annual. Can be grown as cool-season ornamental (Dec-Feb sowing). Self-seeds. More tolerant of warm conditions than L. usitatissimum.
